Hero.tagline:
I paint spaces shaped by human hands,
exploring the quiet presence and memory they hold.
Hero.name: Sylviane Paris

NorthrupStrip.text:
Permanent exhibition at the Northrup King Building · Studio 439 · Minneapolis
Studio 439 · Minneapolis

StatementSection.heading: Artist statement
StatementSection.text1: I am drawn to spaces shaped by human hands — parks, architectural structures, sculptures, and constructed environments that carry intention, care, and time.
StatementSection.text2: My background in theatre design deeply shaped the way I perceive space, light, composition, and atmosphere.
StatementSection.text3: In my paintings, I explore these places not only for their visual presence, but for what they hold beyond it: the quiet imprint of those who created them and those who have passed through them.
StatementSection.text4: Alongside these public spaces, I am also drawn to more intimate scenes — still lifes that reflect a quieter, personal dimension of experience. A gentle celebration of life.

CommissionTeaser.heading: Commission
CommissionTeaser.title: Creating Together
CommissionTeaser.text: A commissioned painting begins with a conversation. It is a process of listening — to a place, a feeling, or a memory someone wishes to preserve. Together, we shape an artwork that becomes personal, meaningful, and deeply connected to its story.

CollectorQuote.quote: We own a painting by Sylviane in our living room — a moment beautifully suspended in time, inspired by our old family bread oven. Every detail feels true. The stone bench, where my grandfather once sat, seems as if it is still waiting for him.

Footer.title: Visit the Studio Gallery
Footer.text: The permanent exhibition is open to the public every Saturday from 12:00 PM to 4:00 PM and by appointment.
Footer.warning: No image on this site may be reproduced without written permission.
Footer.newsletterText: Subscribe to receive updates on new works, exhibitions, and studio news.

About.title: About — Sylviane Paris
About.description: About Sylviane Paris, painter born in France and based in Minneapolis, Minnesota. Theater designer turned fine artist exploring space, light, and memory.
About.heading:
A life shaped by
space, light,
and quiet presence.
About.text1: Born in France and now based in Minneapolis, Sylviane Paris paints places, interiors, and still moments with an attentiveness that comes from years of looking closely at how atmosphere, memory, and form live together.
About.studioSub:
Minneapolis, Minnesota
Painter working between architectural space, gardens, still life, and memory.
About.quote: Her work begins in observation, but it stays for the emotional atmosphere a place leaves behind.
About.bio1: Sylviane's artistic path began in France, where she worked as a theater designer. That early practice sharpened her sensitivity to composition, perspective, and the expressive power of light within constructed space.
About.bio2: Today, that same sensibility continues through painting. Whether depicting a park, a sculpture garden, an interior, or a still life, she is drawn to scenes that hold a sense of presence, memory, and quiet reflection.
About.bio3: Her paintings have been exhibited in France, Denmark, and the United States, and are held in private collections.
About.practiceThemes: Themes: architectural presence, public gardens, interiors, still life, memory.
About.practiceFocus: Focus: atmosphere, perspective, and the quiet relationship between people and place.
About.practiceNote: Earlier works may be signed Sylviane Dickson. All works are by the same artist, Sylviane Paris.
About.foundationText: A background in stage design informs the way space is arranged, lit, and emotionally felt within each composition.
About.perspectiveText: Gardens, structures, and intimate objects become vessels for memory, stillness, and the traces of human presence.
About.studioLifeText: Her studio at Northrup King Building offers a place where visitors can encounter the work closely and in person.
About.statementQuote: I am drawn to spaces shaped by human hands - parks, architectural structures, sculptures, and constructed environments that carry intention, care, and time.
About.statementCol1Para1: In my paintings, I explore these places not only for their visual presence, but for what they hold beyond it: the quiet imprint of those who created them and those who have passed through them.
About.statementCol1Para2: These environments, often familiar and open to all, become sites of contemplation where absence and presence coexist.
About.statementCol1Para3: Alongside these public and shared spaces, I am also drawn to more intimate scenes - still lifes that reflect a quieter, personal dimension of experience. Within these moments, I see a gentle celebration of life, where simple objects and arrangements hold traces of living, time, and attention.
About.statementCol2Para1: Light, perspective, and composition guide my process. I am particularly interested in moments where a shift in angle or atmosphere reveals something subtle - when a place or an object begins to feel inhabited, even in stillness.
About.statementCol2Para2: Through painting, I seek to honor these spaces and moments by capturing their atmosphere and allowing their silent presence to emerge.
About.statementCol2Para3: My work invites a slower way of seeing, where the ordinary becomes quietly significant and where memory - and a quiet celebration of life - can be sensed within both the built environment and intimate spaces.
About.connectTitle:
See the work in person
or begin a conversation.
About.connectText: The studio gallery is located at Northrup King Building, Studio 439, in Minneapolis. For commissions, exhibitions, or general inquiries, the contact page is the best place to reach out.

Collectors.title: Collector Stories — Sylviane Paris
Collectors.description: Words from collectors of Sylviane Paris's work.
Collectors.heading:
Words from those
who carry the work.
Collectors.intro1: Each piece I create holds a presence — of a place, a moment, or a memory quietly lived.
Collectors.intro2: Over time, these works become part of the homes and lives of those who receive them.
Collectors.intro3: Here are a few words from collectors who share that experience.
Collectors.bmText: We own a painting by Sylviane in our living room — a moment beautifully suspended in time, inspired by our old family bread oven. Every detail feels true. The stone bench, where my grandfather once sat, seems as if it is still waiting for him. The 17th-century stones carry a deep sense of memory, evoking emotions from another era. And on the table, the freshly baked bread seems to still release its warm aroma.
Collectors.bmQuote: A moment beautifully suspended in time — the stone bench, where my grandfather once sat, seems as if it is still waiting for him.
Collectors.bmTextFr: Nous possédons un tableau de Sylviane dans notre séjour, véritable vision figée dans le temps, inspirée de notre ancien four à pain familial. Chaque détail est empreint de justesse. Le banc de pierre, où mon grand-père s'asseyait, semble encore attendre son retour. Les pierres du XVIIe siècle, chargées de mémoire, font naître une émotion profonde, comme venue d'une autre époque. Et sur la table, les pains tout juste sortis du four semblent encore en diffuser le parfum.
Collectors.ssText: This painting fills our home with a sense of calm and peace — it truly brings us joy. We will cherish its beauty and artistry for years to come.
Collectors.dmText: I gifted my husband a drawing by Sylviane Paris. She beautifully captured our marsh and the most divine sunset we had ever seen. Thanks to her talent, we are able to relive that magical moment forever.
Collectors.jsText: Sylviane Paris beautifully captured the essence of our home. Looking at the painting brings back all the special moments shared with family and friends, and the love we felt there. It is a treasured keepsake to be enjoyed now and passed on to our children and grandchildren.
Collectors.ctaText: A place, a memory, a moment — translated into something you will keep forever.

Commission.title: Commission — Sylviane Paris
Commission.description: Commission an original painting by Sylviane Paris.
Commission.heading:
Creating
Together
Commission.heroText: Each painting begins with a conversation — about a place, a memory, and what you wish to hold onto.
Commission.bmText: We own a painting by Sylviane in our living room — a moment beautifully suspended in time, inspired by our old family bread oven. Every detail feels true. The stone bench, where my grandfather once sat, seems as if it is still waiting for him.
Commission.aboutHeading:
A place.
A memory.
A painting.
Commission.aboutText1: I create commissioned paintings inspired by meaningful places, spaces, and moments.
Commission.aboutText2: Whether it is a park, a garden, an architectural setting, or a personal environment, I work closely with each client to understand what draws them to the subject and how they wish to experience it through painting.
Commission.aboutText3: Commissions may be based on your own photographs or on images I create specifically for the project.
Commission.aboutText4: Each piece is developed through a thoughtful exchange, allowing the project to take shape with clarity and intention. My aim is to translate not only the visual elements, but also the atmosphere and presence that make a place significant.
Commission.formatText1: Most commissioned works are created in medium formats that allow space for detail, atmosphere, and the perception of light to fully develop.
Commission.formatText2: However, each project is approached individually, and dimensions may vary depending on the subject, composition, and the nature of the reference material.
Commission.formSub: If you have a project in mind, I would be happy to discuss it with you.
Commission.formSubject: Tell me about the place or subject you have in mind
Commission.formSubjectPlaceholder: Describe the place, space, or moment you would like me to paint...
Commission.formTimelinePlaceholder: e.g. No rush · By December · Gift for June
Commission.formFindUsPlaceholder: Instagram · Word of mouth · Northrup King Building · Other

Series.title: Series — Sylviane Paris
Series.description: Collections of paintings by Sylviane Paris.
Series.heading: Series

architecture-of-light.name: Architecture of Light
architecture-of-light.subtitle: Sacred & contemplative spaces
architecture-of-light.description: Architecture of Light is an exploration of what people have built, and what remains of them through time. I look at architectural and sculptural forms not as objects, but as traces of human presence that continue to exist. Through light, these structures are revealed again — gently transformed into atmosphere, memory, and perception. My work is an act of attention and respect toward what has been made and left behind, where light becomes a way of seeing what still lives within form.

inner-illumination.title: Inner Illumination
inner-illumination.hint: Inner Illumination is inspired by the Basilica of Saint Mary in Minnesota and based on my own photograph. This work captures the quiet dialogue between structure and light. As illumination moves through the space, architecture becomes not only visible, but deeply felt.

divine-presence.name: Divine Presence
divine-presence.subtitle: Form, sculpture & quiet encounter
divine-presence.description: Divine Presence explores moments where form carries a sense of something beyond the visible. Whether emerging through sculpture, landscape, or quiet spaces, these works reflect a perception of presence that feels both human and beyond human. Light, form, and atmosphere come together to suggest a subtle spiritual dimension — where what is seen opens into what is felt.

morning-visit.title: Morning Visit
morning-visit.hint: A quiet encounter between figure, architecture, and stillness, where presence unfolds in silence.

souls-of-the-park.title: The Souls of the Park
souls-of-the-park.hint: Inspired by my experience as a theatre designer working with neutral masks for actors, The Souls of the Park reflects on how still forms come alive through presence and perception. In stone figures set within nature, I see a similar transformation — where what is silent becomes inhabited.

living-moments.name: Living Moments
living-moments.subtitle: Intimate spaces & everyday beauty
living-moments.description: Living Moments explores the quiet beauty of everyday life within intimate spaces. These works focus on simple scenes shaped by human presence — objects, gestures, and arrangements that carry warmth, memory, and a sense of lived experience. Through light and atmosphere, ordinary moments are transformed into spaces of attention, where what is simple becomes quietly meaningful.

art-of-indulgence.title: The Art of Indulgence
art-of-indulgence.hint: A quiet celebration of enjoyment in everyday life, where simple gestures and objects carry warmth and presence.

timeless-craft.title: Timeless Craft
timeless-craft.hint: Originally built in the 17th century and later acquired by my ancestor, this piece now lives as a space for gathering and celebrating important family moments, where history and present life quietly overlap.

balcony-refreshment.title: Balcony Refreshment
balcony-refreshment.hint: A suspended moment between interior life and horizon, shaped by light, air, and stillness.

enchanted-path.name: Enchanted Path
enchanted-path.subtitle: Nature as passage & transformation
enchanted-path.description: Enchanted Path explores nature as a place of passage, transformation, and quiet mystery. These works are not only landscapes, but experiences of movement — where colour, light, and form invite a sense of transition between the visible and the imagined. Each path suggests a journey, both physical and inward.

whispering-passage.title: Whispering Passage
whispering-passage.hint: A journey through nature as a place of transition and quiet mystery, where form, light, and color suggest movement between the visible and the imagined.

path-of-wonder.title: The Path of Wonder
path-of-wonder.hint: A winding path disappears beneath a canopy of luminous foliage, inviting the viewer into a landscape of quiet discovery. Bathed in soft light and vibrant color, the forest becomes a place where imagination and memory meet. This painting explores the sense of wonder that can emerge when we slow our pace and allow ourselves to wander. The path leads beyond the visible destination, suggesting a journey of curiosity, reflection, and possibility.

studies.name: Studies
studies.subtitle: Early works & explorations
studies.description: Studies brings together early works and explorations created through direct reference. These pieces reflect a stage of learning and observation that contributed to the development of my current practice, now rooted in my own photographic sources.

poseidon.title: Poseidon, The Magnificent
poseidon.hint: An early exploration of form, strength, and sculptural presence.

natures-guardians.title: Nature's Guardians
natures-guardians.hint: A study of protective figures emerging within natural forms.

beauty-of-venus.title: The Beauty of Venus
beauty-of-venus.hint: A study of classical form and timeless presence, where light reveals the softness and quiet vitality of the figure.
